title: Fermented Pecorino Crumble
description: A light and nutritious Norwegian dish featuring pecorino cheese that is fermented and crumbly in texture. This recipe is designed to be sous-vide cooked and is also fodmap-friendly.
instructions: Chop the pecorino cheese into small cubes and mix with a bit of sugar-free sweetener (if using) and salt.
Place the cheese mixture in a sous-vive bag and seal it tightly. Vacuum sealing is optional but recommended for even cooking.
Cook the sealed sous-vide bag for 1 hour at 65°C.
Let cool down to room temperature, then use your fingers or a fork to break up the cheese into a crumbly consistency.
Store in an airtight container and refrigerate until ready to serve.
